title: Bridge Essay: Multimodal neurophenomenology of advanced concentration absorption meditation: A

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Formless/Absorption** (Phase 8) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Formless/Absorption to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Multimodal neurophenomenology of advanced concentration absorption meditation: An intensively sampled case study of Jhana.** — Neuroimage. PMID: 39681243. DOI: 10.1016/j.neuroimage.2024.120973. Using acombination of fMRI, EEG, and phenomenology ratings, we examined the neurophenomenology of advanced concentrative absorption meditation, namely jhanas (ACAM-J), in apractitioner with over 23,000 hof meditation practice. Our study shows that ACAM-Jstates induce reliable changes in conscious experience and that these experiences are related to neural activity.
